case_study_exemption_reason: Cross-repo tooling chore — child of parent_feature ios-code-connect (also no_case_study_required). Workflow + scaffold script + CI integration are documented in docs/design-system/ios-code-connect-workflow.md (parent's T5 deliverable) and the workflow YAML itself; a separate case study would duplicate that content.
isolation_opt_out_reason: Cross-repo tooling chore — touches scripts/ + .claude/skills/ + .github/workflows/ in BOTH FT2 and fitme-story. Branch isolation gate's Mode B (infra paths) fires by design; opt-out lets the chore proceed without per-commit auto-isolation, but commits remain on dedicated chore/* branches per repo for review hygiene.

implementation.note: 3-layer automation closing the loop on ios-code-connect (PR #277) + fitme-story T20 (PR #75): A) scaffold script auto-generates mapping files from state.json::figma_node_ids; B) /design build skill calls scaffold after node ID capture; C) CI workflow auto-publishes on merge.
implementation.progress: 4/5 tasks done; T5 = end-to-end test on next real new UI feature (gate)

tasks.title: Layer A — scripts/scaffold-figma-mapping.py (FT2/iOS) + scripts/scaffold-figma-mapping.mjs (fitme-story/web)
tasks.scope_note: Layer A scaffold scripts shipped: scripts/scaffold-figma-mapping.py via FT2 PR #279 (iOS) + scripts/scaffold-figma-mapping.mjs via fitme-story PR #77 (web). Both read state.json::figma_node_ids, coalesce multi-state variants per file, idempotent (--force overrides existing), code_mapping override block for keys that don't match snake_case→PascalCase heuristic.

tasks.title: Layer A docs + first PR
tasks.scope_note: Docs included inline in script docstrings + the Layer A PRs. Companion sections added to docs/skills/design.md, docs/skills/README.md, docs/skills/evolution.md (§27), docs/architecture/dev-guide-v1-to-v7-7.md (§15A), and docs/design-system/figma-code-sync-status.md (Code Connect Verification Contract section) via the docs rollup PR.

tasks.title: Layer B — extend /design build skill to invoke scaffold-figma-mapping after node ID capture
tasks.scope_note: /design build skill extended via FT2 PR #280 (Layer B). After state.json::figma_node_ids is populated in Step 4, the skill auto-invokes scripts/scaffold-figma-mapping.{py|mjs} (auto-detects active repo). Frontmatter description updated to mention Layer B. Bundled with two new mechanical gates: Code Connect write-access gate (/design preflight Step 3.5) + spec ↔ build parity check (/design pre-merge-review Step 3.5).

tasks.title: Layer C — CI publish workflows (one per repo)
tasks.scope_note: CI publish workflows shipped: FT2 .github/workflows/figma-code-connect-publish.yml + fitme-story equivalent. Both gated on FIGMA_ACCESS_TOKEN repo secret (operator added 2026-05-10T06:38–06:39Z; verified via gh api). Web: ubuntu runner + npx figma connect publish. iOS: macos-15 runner + SPM cache + npx figma connect publish with figma.config.json::swiftPackagePath pointing at .figma-cc-tools/Package.swift SPM wrapper subdir (the npm CLI subprocesses to swift run figma-swift to parse Swift files). PR #281 first version was ubuntu-only + npx-only (fell back to html parser, scanned 14817 files); PR #283 (squash 8536fb8) replaced with the SPM wrapper approach.

tasks.title: End-to-end test on a real new UI feature
tasks.scope_note: Pick a near-future UI feature; verify /design build → state.json::figma_node_ids capture → scaffold script auto-runs → .figma.{swift|tsx} files appear → CI publish fires → mappings show in Figma Dev Mode. Documents any rough edges.

metrics.baseline_note: Today (post-PR #277 + #75): operator hand-authors mapping file + manually runs publish
metrics.target_note: After Layer C ships: operator does nothing — mapping scaffolds + publish both fire automatically
metrics.kill_criteria: scaffold script produces wrong/non-compiling files OR CI publish fails > 1x without obvious operator-fixable cause
metrics.progress: Pre-Layer-C: manual_steps=2. Post-Layer-C (today): manual_steps=0 once a UI feature actually flows through. T5 measures.

_meta.scaffold_origin: User answer to 'Automate Code Connect for new UI features?' on 2026-05-10 — chose 'All 3 layers (A + B + C)'. Surfaced as follow-up to ios-code-connect T1-T5 wrap-up + the question 'is the cmd automated when building a new ui feature' — answer was 'no' across both web + iOS, this feature closes that gap.
